lessonTitle: Unit 6: Section 3: Neurosis ................................................................................    18: be aware that anxiety is the basis of all neurosis

targetOutcomes.title: be aware that anxiety is the basis of all neurosis

prerequisiteKnowledge: understand the nature of emotions and how they effect our lives
misconceptions: Students may use Neurosis as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.

sourceReferences.resourceTitle: ALBERTA DISTANCE LEARNING CENTRE SSN2171 Personal Psychology 20 KEY
sourceReferences.whySelected: Defines the exact performance demand students must meet.

Personal Psychology 20  Assignment Booklet 6

ADLC Personal Psychology 20  5

 _D__ 22.

sourceReferences.whySelected: Defines unit scope, outcome language, or required concepts.
sourceReferences.exampleSnippet:
Neurosis:  Mentally-Based Reactions
• Amnesia:  “forgetting”, which usually means personal memories and even self-
identity can be forgotten due to severe mental problems linked to emotions
The text also explains the fascinating phenomenon of fugue amnesia.
• Hypochondria:  falsely believing many illnesses are bodily present after reading
about or hearing about them
For example, someone sees a medical report on TV about brain cancer and is
convinced he or she has it.
• Obsessions:  constant, re-occurring thoughts that are irrational
• Compulsions:  actions that an individual seems compelled to do repeatedly, often
against the his or her will
• Tourette’s Syndrome:  a compulsive condition manifesting itself in the form of
motor tics (eye blinking, shoulder shrugging) or vocal tics (snorting, clearly the
throat)
Neurosis:  Emotionally-Based Reactions
• Phobias:  excessive, irrational fears
Phobias might arise from three situations:
◦   a trauma the individual personally experiences
◦   watching others react with fear and copying their behaviours
◦   misinterpreting data about a situation
Many interesting phobias are listed in the text, page 202.
